label: Abraham: Ur to Canaan
description: The founding migration — Terah's household leaves Ur, pauses at Haran, then Abraham completes the journey to Canaan at God's call.

label: Abraham: Canaan to Egypt and Back
description: Driven by famine, Abraham descends to Egypt where Pharaoh takes Sarai. After the plagues Pharaoh sends them away and Abraham returns north.

label: Abraham and Lot Separate
description: After strife between their herdsmen, Abraham lets Lot choose the Jordan plain. Abraham moves to Hebron.

label: Abraham Rescues Lot
description: Abraham pursues the four kings who captured Lot all the way to Dan, defeats them by night, and returns through Salem where Melchizedek blesses him.

label: Abraham: Journey to Mount Moriah
description: The three-day journey from Beersheba to offer Isaac on Mount Moriah — the supreme test of faith. God provides the ram. Abraham names the place 'The Lord Will Provide.'

label: Abraham at Gerar
description: Abraham moves to Gerar and again passes Sarah off as his sister before Abimelech. He then digs wells at Beersheba and makes a covenant.

label: Hagar: Wilderness Flights
description: Twice Hagar flees into the wilderness — first when pregnant with Ishmael, then when expelled with the boy. God meets her both times.

label: Isaac: Gerar to Beersheba
description: Isaac sojourns in Gerar during famine, again passes Rebekah as his sister, digs wells, and finally settles at Beersheba where God appears to him.

label: Jacob: Fleeing to Haran
description: Jacob flees Esau's wrath, sleeps at Bethel and sees the ladder of angels, then continues to Paddan-Aram. He serves Laban for twenty years.

label: Jacob: Return from Haran
description: Jacob departs secretly from Laban, is pursued and reconciled at Gilead, wrestles at Peniel, meets Esau, and settles at Shechem then Bethel.

label: Jacob: Descent to Egypt
description: At God's word Jacob takes his whole household from Beersheba down to Egypt to be reunited with Joseph. He stops at Beersheba for a final vision.

label: Joseph: Canaan to Egypt (as slave)
description: Joseph is thrown into a pit at Dothan by his brothers, sold to Ishmaelite traders, and taken down to Egypt as a slave.

label: Joseph: Prison to Palace
description: Joseph rises from Potiphar's house to prison to the throne room of Pharaoh, all within the confines of Egypt.

label: Moses: Egypt to Midian
description: Moses flees Egypt after killing an Egyptian taskmaster. He sits at a well in Midian where he meets Jethro's daughters and eventually marries Zipporah.

label: Moses: Return to Egypt
description: After the burning bush encounter at Sinai, Moses returns to Egypt with his wife and sons to confront Pharaoh.

label: The Exodus: Egypt to Sinai
description: Israel departs from Rameses, crosses the sea, and journeys through the wilderness to Mount Sinai. The foundational journey of Israelite identity.

label: Sinai to Kadesh Barnea
description: Israel departs Sinai for Canaan but the spies' negative report at Kadesh Barnea triggers forty years of wandering.

label: Forty Years of Wilderness Wandering
description: The generation condemned to die in the wilderness wanders between Kadesh, the Sinai, and Edom before finally reaching the plains of Moab.

label: Moses: Plains of Moab to Mount Nebo
description: Moses' final journey — from the plains of Moab to the summit of Mount Nebo where God shows him the promised land before his death.

label: Joshua: Central Campaign
description: Israel crosses the Jordan, camps at Gilgal, captures Jericho and Ai, and receives the Gibeonite deception.

label: Joshua: Southern Campaign
description: The sun stands still over Gibeon as Joshua defeats the five Amorite kings and sweeps through the southern hill country.

label: Joshua: Northern Campaign
description: Joshua defeats the northern coalition led by Jabin of Hazor at the Waters of Merom and burns Hazor.

label: Deborah and Barak: Battle of Kishon
description: Deborah summons Barak from Kedesh. Israel musters at Mount Tabor and sweeps down on Sisera's chariot army at the Kishon river.

label: Gideon: Defeat of Midian
description: Gideon musters forces at the spring of Harod, reduces his army to 300, routs the Midianites at night, and pursues them across the Jordan.

label: Samson: Life and Journeys
description: Samson's turbulent life moves between Zorah, Timnah, Gaza, the Valley of Sorek, and finally back to Gaza where he dies.

label: Ruth and Naomi: Moab to Bethlehem
description: Naomi returns from Moab after her husband and sons die. Ruth refuses to leave her. They journey to Bethlehem at the beginning of the barley harvest.

label: Jephthah: Ammonite Campaign
description: Jephthah passes through Ammon and Moab, vows his vow, and defeats the Ammonites — at the cost of his daughter.

label: Samuel's Annual Circuit
description: Samuel judged Israel on an annual circuit from his home in Ramah through Bethel, Gilgal, and Mizpah.

label: Samuel Anoints Saul
description: Saul searching for lost donkeys encounters Samuel at Ramah, is privately anointed, then publicly acclaimed king at Mizpah and confirmed at Gilgal.

label: Samuel Anoints David
description: God sends Samuel from Ramah to Bethlehem to anoint the youngest son of Jesse as king.

label: Saul: Military Campaigns
description: Saul's major campaigns: relief of Jabesh Gilead, defeat of the Philistines at Michmash, war with the Amalekites, and final battle at Gilboa.

label: Saul Pursues David
description: Saul's increasingly frantic pursuit of David across the Judean wilderness — from Gibeah to Ramah, Nob, the Shephelah, En-Gedi, and the Wilderness of Ziph.

label: David: From Shepherd to Court
description: David's trajectory from Bethlehem shepherd to Saul's court musician to giant-killer — from the Elah Valley to the palace at Gibeah.

label: David: Fugitive from Saul
description: David's years as a fugitive: from Gibeah through Nob, Gath, Adullam, Keilah, Ziph, En-Gedi, and finally to Philistine territory at Ziklag.

label: David: Hebron to Jerusalem
description: David is crowned at Hebron over Judah, then all Israel after Ishbosheth's death. He captures Jerusalem from the Jebusites and makes it his capital.

label: David: Flight from Absalom
description: David flees Jerusalem barefoot across the Kidron and over the Mount of Olives to Mahanaim in Gilead as Absalom takes the city.

label: David: Ark Brought to Jerusalem
description: David brings the ark from Kiriath Jearim to Jerusalem — with a terrifying interruption at the threshing floor of Nacon before the joyful procession into the city.

label: David: Ammonite and Aramean Wars
description: David's general Joab fights Ammon while David stays in Jerusalem — leading to the Bathsheba incident. Joab eventually captures Rabbah.

label: Solomon: To Gibeon for Wisdom
description: The young Solomon goes to the great high place at Gibeon to sacrifice; God appears in a dream and grants him wisdom.

label: Queen of Sheba to Jerusalem
description: The Queen of Sheba travels from South Arabia (Yemen) northward along the incense route to test Solomon with hard questions and exchange lavish gifts.

label: Elijah: Cherith and Zarephath
description: Elijah flees Ahab after announcing the drought. God directs him east to the Wadi Cherith, then north to the widow of Zarephath in Sidon where he raises her son.

label: Elijah: Carmel to Horeb
description: After the fire on Carmel Elijah flees Jezebel's threat south through Beersheba into the wilderness. An angel feeds him twice. He walks forty days to Horeb where God speaks in the still small voice.

label: Elijah Calls Elisha
description: Returning from Horeb, Elijah finds Elisha ploughing at Abel Meholah and throws his mantle over him.

label: Elijah: Journey to Ascension
description: Elijah and Elisha's final journey together from Gilgal through Bethel and Jericho to the Jordan, where Elijah is taken up in the whirlwind.

label: Elisha: Ministry Circuit
description: Elisha's prophetic ministry takes him across Israel — from the Jordan crossing to Jericho, Bethel, Carmel, Shunem, Dothan, Samaria, and into Aram.

label: Naaman: Damascus to Samaria
description: The Aramean commander Naaman travels from Damascus to Elisha in Samaria seeking healing from leprosy. Elisha tells him to wash in the Jordan seven times.

label: Jonah: Flight and Mission
description: Jonah flees from God's call to Nineveh by boarding a ship at Joppa bound for Tarshish. After the storm and the great fish, he is vomited ashore and goes to Nineveh.

label: Jeremiah: Jerusalem to Egypt
description: After Jerusalem's fall and Gedaliah's assassination, the remaining people force Jeremiah to accompany them to Egypt against God's word. He ends his days at Tahpanhes.

label: Ezekiel: Jerusalem to Babylon
description: Ezekiel was deported with King Jehoiachin in 597 BC, the first wave of Babylonian exiles. He ministered among the exiles at Tel Abib on the Chebar canal near Nippur.

label: Daniel: Jerusalem to Babylon
description: Daniel is deported from Jerusalem to Babylon as a young man in 605 BC under Nebuchadnezzar. He serves there through Babylonian and Persian regimes for over 70 years.

label: Daniel: Vision at Susa
description: Daniel receives his vision of the ram and goat while at Susa (possibly in a visionary transport) by the Ulai canal.

label: Zerubbabel: Return from Exile
description: The first return under Zerubbabel and Jeshua — nearly 50,000 exiles travel from Babylon along the Euphrates route northwest then south into Canaan to Jerusalem.

label: Ezra: Return from Babylon
description: Ezra leads the second return from Babylon to Jerusalem in 458 BC with about 1,500 men. He assembles the group at the Ahava canal and arrives after a four-month journey.

label: Nehemiah: Susa to Jerusalem
description: Nehemiah receives news of Jerusalem's broken walls in Susa. Artaxerxes grants him leave. He travels to Jerusalem and leads the wall's rebuilding in 52 days.

label: Esther: Life in Susa
description: Esther's entire story takes place within the citadel of Susa — from her initial presentation to the palace through the reversal of Haman's decree.

label: Magi: Journey to Bethlehem
description: Wise men from the east follow the star to Jerusalem, then to Bethlehem. Warned in a dream, they return home by another route.

label: Holy Family: Flight to Egypt
description: Warned in a dream, Joseph takes Mary and the infant Jesus to Egypt to escape Herod's massacre. After Herod's death they return and settle in Nazareth.

label: Jesus: Baptism and Temptation
description: Jesus comes from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptised by John. Immediately the Spirit drives him into the wilderness for forty days of temptation.

label: Jesus: Galilean Ministry
description: Jesus moves from Nazareth to make Capernaum his base, calling disciples from the lake shore and teaching throughout the Galilean synagogues.

label: Jesus: Withdrawal to Tyre and Sidon
description: Jesus withdraws to the region of Tyre and Sidon where a Syrophoenician woman seeks healing for her daughter. He returns through the Decapolis.

label: Jesus: To Caesarea Philippi
description: Jesus takes his disciples north to Caesarea Philippi — Peter's confession, the first prediction of the Passion, and the Transfiguration on the mountain.

label: Jesus: Through Samaria
description: Jesus passes through Samaria and stops at Jacob's well in Sychar where he speaks with the Samaritan woman and stays two days.

label: Jesus: Jerusalem Festival Visits
description: Jesus makes multiple visits to Jerusalem for feasts — healing at Bethesda (John 5), the Feast of Tabernacles (John 7–8), Hanukkah (John 10), raising Lazarus (John 11).

label: Jesus: Perea and Raising Lazarus
description: Jesus withdraws across the Jordan to Perea, receives news of Lazarus's illness, waits two days, then returns to raise him in Bethany.

label: Jesus: Final Journey to Jerusalem
description: Jesus makes his determined final journey from Galilee through Perea, stops at Jericho where he heals blind Bartimaeus and eats with Zacchaeus, and arrives at Bethany.

label: Jesus: Passion Week in Jerusalem
description: The final week — triumphal entry, Temple cleansing, Olivet Discourse, Last Supper in the upper room, Gethsemane, arrest, trials before Caiaphas and Pilate, crucifixion at Golgotha.

label: Jesus: Resurrection Appearances
description: The risen Jesus appears in Jerusalem, on the road to Emmaus, by the Sea of Galilee, on a mountain in Galilee, and finally ascends from the Mount of Olives.

label: John the Baptist: Wilderness Ministry
description: John preaches in the Judean wilderness along the Jordan, baptising at Bethany beyond the Jordan and at Aenon near Salim. He is imprisoned at Machaerus.

label: Peter: Coastal Ministry
description: Peter travels the coastal plain — healing Aeneas in Lydda, raising Dorcas in Joppa, receiving his vision on a rooftop, and visiting the centurion Cornelius in Caesarea.

label: Peter: Visit to Antioch
description: Peter visits the church at Antioch where Paul later confronts him publicly for withdrawing from Gentile table fellowship under pressure from James's delegation.

label: Philip: Samaria and Ethiopian Eunuch
description: Philip flees Jerusalem persecution and preaches in Samaria with great effect. An angel redirects him to the Gaza road where he baptises the Ethiopian court official, then is transported to Azotus.

label: Paul: Damascus Road Conversion
description: Saul travels from Jerusalem to Damascus to arrest Christians. The risen Jesus appears to him in blinding light. He is led into Damascus, fasts three days, then is baptised by Ananias.

label: Paul: Damascus, Arabia, Jerusalem
description: After conversion Paul goes to Arabia (likely Nabataean territory near Petra), returns to Damascus for three years, then visits Jerusalem for fifteen days to meet Peter and James.

label: Paul's First Missionary Journey
description: Sent out from Antioch, Paul and Barnabas sail to Cyprus, cross the island, sail to Pamphylia, and journey inland through Pisidia and Lycaonia before retracing their steps.

label: Paul: Jerusalem Council
description: Paul and Barnabas travel from Antioch to Jerusalem for the council that settles the Gentile circumcision question, then return with Silas and Judas Barsabbas.

label: Paul's Second Missionary Journey
description: Paul and Silas go overland through Syria and Cilicia, pick up Timothy in Lystra, cross to Macedonia following the vision, plant churches in Philippi, Thessalonica, Berea, Athens, and Corinth.

label: Paul's Third Missionary Journey
description: Paul spends three years in Ephesus as his Asian base, then travels through Macedonia and Greece, returns by land through Macedonia, crosses to Troas, and coasts down to Miletus and Jerusalem.

label: Paul's Voyage to Rome
description: Paul sails as a prisoner from Caesarea, coasting past Cyprus and Anatolia, through the storm and shipwreck at Malta, then up through Sicily and Italy to Rome.

label: Paul: Post-Acts Travels (Tradition)
description: Based on the Pastoral Epistles, Paul may have been released from Roman imprisonment and travelled to Spain, Crete, Ephesus, Macedonia, Nicopolis, and Troas before his second arrest and execution in Rome.

label: Barnabas: Cyprus with Mark
description: After separating from Paul over John Mark, Barnabas takes Mark and sails to Cyprus — his home island — to continue missionary work there.

label: Apollos: Alexandria to Corinth
description: Apollos comes from Alexandria to Ephesus, preaches boldly, is instructed more fully by Priscilla and Aquila, then crosses to Achaia and Corinth where he powerfully refutes the Jews.

label: John the Apostle: Ephesus and Patmos
description: According to strong early tradition, John settled in Ephesus in his later years and was exiled to the island of Patmos where he received the Revelation. He was released and returned to Ephesus.

label: John Mark: Ministry Journey
description: Mark's journeys from Jerusalem with Barnabas and Paul, his return from Perga, his later work with Barnabas in Cyprus, and his eventual reconciliation with Paul.

label: Titus: Ministry in Corinth and Crete
description: Titus served as Paul's troubleshooter in Corinth during the painful letter period, was left in Crete to appoint elders, and was summoned to Nicopolis.

label: Joseph's Brothers: Canaan to Egypt
description: Joseph's ten brothers make multiple trips to Egypt during the famine to buy grain, eventually bringing Benjamin and then the whole family.

label: Balaam: Mesopotamia to Moab
description: Balak king of Moab summons Balaam from Pethor in Mesopotamia to curse Israel. God turns the curses to blessings. Balaam's donkey speaks on the road.

label: Joshua's Spies: Jericho Reconnaissance
description: Joshua sends two spies secretly to Jericho. They stay with Rahab who hides them. They report back that the people's hearts have melted.

label: Caleb: Claiming Hebron
description: At age 85, Caleb claims the mountain God promised him forty-five years earlier when he gave a faithful spy report. He drives out the Anakim from Hebron.

label: Levite and Concubine: Road to Gibeah
description: A Levite from Ephraim retrieves his concubine from Bethlehem and travels home. They stop at Gibeah of Benjamin where the horrific outrage occurs that triggers a civil war.

label: Hannah: Annual Pilgrimage to Shiloh
description: Elkanah's family makes their annual pilgrimage from Ramah to Shiloh. Hannah prays desperately for a son. After Samuel's birth she fulfils her vow and brings him to Eli.

label: Ark of the Covenant: Philistine Journey
description: The captured ark travels from Ebenezer to Ashdod, then to Gath, to Ekron — causing plagues everywhere — before being returned on a cart to Beth Shemesh and Kiriath Jearim.

label: Ezra: Jerusalem Reform Ministry
description: Ezra's ministry in Jerusalem — reading the Law at the Water Gate, leading the great assembly of repentance, and reforming the mixed marriages.

label: Nehemiah: Midnight Survey of Jerusalem Walls
description: Three days after arriving, Nehemiah makes his secret night inspection of Jerusalem's broken walls before announcing the rebuilding project.

label: Hezekiah: Defense of Jerusalem
description: As Assyria advances, Hezekiah cuts Siloam's tunnel, reinforces Jerusalem's walls, and receives Isaiah's prophecy that the city will be spared.

label: Josiah: Covenant Renewal and Death
description: Josiah's reform sweeps from Jerusalem through Bethel and the cities of Samaria, desecrating all the high places. He later dies at Megiddo fighting Pharaoh Necho.
